question.text: Under Section 2(31) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, which of the following is NOT included in the definition of 'Person'?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 2(31) defines 'Person' to include seven categories: (i) Individual, (ii) Hindu Undivided Family (HUF), (iii) Company, (iv) Firm or LLP, (v) Association of Persons (AOP) or Body of Individuals (BOI), (vi) Local authority, and (vii) Artificial juridical person. A 'partnership deed' is a legal document — not an entity — and is therefore not a 'Person' under the Act. A 'Firm' is the entity; the deed merely governs its terms. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse 'Firm' with 'partnership deed' since firms are governed by partnership deeds. HUF and AOP are less commonly encountered and might seem unusual, but both are explicitly listed. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A also tests the definition of 'Person' for understanding taxation of different entities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When onboarding clients, correctly identifying the 'person' category is essential — it determines applicable tax rates, deductions, and compliance requirements.
